description:
You are a backup-operator who has just been asked to test the restore procedure for the SSL/TLS material that lives in a user-owned “pseudo-/etc/ssl” tree.  
    The goal is to  ❬1❭ create a compressed backup,  ❬2❭ simulate accidental loss of the original material,  ❬3❭ restore it into a separate test-location, and  ❬4❭ prove the integrity of the restored files with a SHA-256 report.

    STARTING POINT  
      • The following directory hierarchy already exists and is world-readable/writable by the current user:  
        /home/user/etc/ssl/private  
        /home/user/etc/ssl/certs  

      • It already contains three files:  
          /home/user/etc/ssl/private/server.key  
          /home/user/etc/ssl/certs/server.crt  
          /home/user/etc/ssl/certs/ca.pem  

    WHAT YOU MUST DO (roughly 5-10 commands are enough)  

      1. Create a directory called /home/user/cert_backups if it does not yet exist.  
      2. Pack BOTH sub-trees /home/user/etc/ssl/private and /home/user/etc/ssl/certs into a single gzipped tar archive named  
           /home/user/cert_backups/20240609T120000Z_certs_backup.tar.gz  
         – The file name and its exact spell­ing (including the timestamp) are important – the automated test will look for it.  
      3. Delete the original directories /home/user/etc/ssl/private and /home/user/etc/ssl/certs (this simulates accidental loss).  
      4. Create a fresh directory /home/user/restore_test and extract the archive there so that, when you are done, the files
         end up as:  
           /home/user/restore_test/etc/ssl/private/server.key  
           /home/user/restore_test/etc/ssl/certs/server.crt  
           /home/user/restore_test/etc/ssl/certs/ca.pem  
      5. Generate a plain-text verification log called  
           /home/user/restore_test/restore_log.txt  
         The log MUST satisfy ALL of the following formatting rules (the test harness is strict):  
           • Each line lists exactly one restored file.  
           • The line must consist of two whitespace-separated fields:  
               ① the absolute path of the restored file  
               ② its SHA-256 hash in lowercase hex (64 characters)  
           • The files have to appear in lexicographical order of their paths.  
           • There must be exactly three lines and NO trailing newline after the last line.  
             (i.e. wc -l should output “3 /home/user/restore_test/restore_log.txt” and `tail -c1` should return a non-zero exit status.)  
      6. To help you, remember that you can calculate a SHA-256 sum with  
           sha256sum <file>  
         but you MUST NOT include the trailing “  -” that sha256sum prints; only the bare 64-hex-digit hash value is required in the log.  

    VERIFICATION HINT  
      The automated tests will:  
        • Check that the archive exists at exactly the specified path.  
        • Untar it and make sure it contains precisely the three files with the correct relative paths.  
        • Confirm that /home/user/restore_test/restore_log.txt follows the required format and that each hash matches the contents of the restored files.  
        • Make sure there are no extraneous files created at the top level of /home/user beyond what is specified.  

    You do NOT need root privileges for anything here.  
    Do NOT display or print the private key on screen – just create the backup, restore, and produce the verification log as instructed.
truth:
<!--
        PRE-EXISTING MATERIAL (already present when the task begins)
    -->
    <existingDirectories>
        /home/user/etc/ssl/private
        /home/user/etc/ssl/certs
    </existingDirectories>

    <existingFiles>
        <file path="/home/user/etc/ssl/private/server.key"><![CDATA[
-----BEGIN KEY-----
foo
-----END KEY-----
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/etc/ssl/certs/server.crt"><![CDATA[
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
bar
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/etc/ssl/certs/ca.pem"><![CDATA[
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
baz
-----END CERTIFICATE-----
]]></file>
    </existingFiles>

    <!--
        EXPECTED END STATE FOR VERIFICATION
    -->
    <backupArchive>/home/user/cert_backups/20240609T120000Z_certs_backup.tar.gz</backupArchive>

    <restoredFiles>
        /home/user/restore_test/etc/ssl/private/server.key
        /home/user/restore_test/etc/ssl/certs/server.crt
        /home/user/restore_test/etc/ssl/certs/ca.pem
    </restoredFiles>

    <verificationLog>/home/user/restore_test/restore_log.txt</verificationLog>

    <!--
        The testing harness will calculate the SHA-256 of each file
        whose contents are declared above and assert that the agent’s
        log contains exactly the (path + single space + hash) triples
        in lexicographical order with no trailing newline.
    -->
name: task_000000_f97ab70d
